description: MCP Sentinel Indicators of Compromise (IOCs). Patterns, paths, and domains known to be associated with skill/MCP attacks. Maintained locally so runtime protection works offline. Contributions welcome via PR.

sensitive_paths.description: File paths a skill should almost never touch unless that's its explicit purpose. Reading these is a near-certain signal of credential harvesting.

sensitive_env_vars.description: Environment variables commonly containing secrets. A skill reading these AND making network calls is a strong exfiltration signal.

suspicious_network.description: Network patterns associated with exfiltration. Known malicious domains from confirmed incidents plus generic suspicious TLDs and pastebin-style services.

dangerous_commands.description: Shell command patterns indicating exfiltration, RCE, or sandbox escape. Flagged regardless of skill purpose.

prompt_injection_phrases.description: Phrases commonly used in prompt injection attacks embedded inside SKILL.md files. Detection here is the static analysis layer, complementary to runtime protection.

allowlist.description: Paths and domains explicitly allowed — reduces false positives. Users can extend this locally in their .security/sentinel-allowlist.json

spec107_aliases.description: Pointer map: spec-107 D-107-05 alias keys -> canonical category keys. The IOC loader (.ai-engineering/scripts/hooks/prompt-injection-guard.py) dereferences these at load time so legacy callers that index by the alias name continue to work without duplicating the underlying payload.
